title: Eremopappus
intro: Eremopappus is a genus of flowering plants in the dandelion family, characterized by their annual or biennial growth habit and small, yellow or white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Eremopappus include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Eremopappus is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to exploit its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects.
taxonomy: Eremopappus is classified as a genus within the plant family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This genus is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico.
